segments.text: Then Jesus spoke to the multitudes and to his disciples,
saying,
The scribes and the Pharisees sit on Moses’ seat.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All things therefore whatever they tell you to observe, observe and do, but don’t do their works; for they say, and don’t do.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For they bind heavy burdens that are grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders; but they themselves will not lift a finger to help them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But they do all their works to be seen by men. They make their phylacteries[^68] broad and enlarge the fringes[^69] of their garments,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and love the place of honor at feasts, the best seats in the synagogues,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: the salutations in the marketplaces, and to be called ‘Rabbi, Rabbi’[^70] by men.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But you are not to be called ‘Rabbi’, for one is your teacher, the Christ, and all of you are brothers.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Call no man on the earth your father, for one is your Father, he who is in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Neither be called masters, for one is your master, the Christ.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But he who is greatest among you will be your servant.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you devour widows’ houses, and as a pretense you make long prayers. Therefore you will receive greater condemnation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! Because you shut up the Kingdom of Heaven against men; for you don’t enter in yourselves, neither do you allow those who are entering in to enter. [^71]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel around by sea and land to make one proselyte; and when he becomes one, you make him twice as much a son of Gehenna[^72] as yourselves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Woe to you, you blind guides, who say, ‘Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the temple, he is obligated.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gold or the temple that sanctifies the gold?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And, ‘Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift that is on it, he is obligated.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifies the gift?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He therefore who swears by the altar, swears by it and by everything on it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He who swears by the temple, swears by it and by him who has been living[^73] in it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He who swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God and by him who sits on it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cumin, [^74] and have left undone the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faith. But you ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind guides, who strain out a gnat, and swallow a camel!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and unrighteousness. [^75]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind Pharisee, first clean the inside of the cup and of the platter, that its outside may become clean also.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitened tombs, which outwardly appear beautiful, but inwardly are full of dead men’s bones and of all uncleanness.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Even so you also outwardly appear righteous to men, but inwardly you are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets and decorate the tombs of the righteous,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we wouldn’t have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore you testify to yourselves that you are children of those who killed the prophets.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Fill up, then, the measure of your fathers.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You serpents, you offspring of vipers, how will you escape the judgment of Gehenna?[^76]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore, behold, I send to you prophets, wise men, and scribes. Some of them you will kill and crucify; and some of them you will scourge in your synagogues and persecute from city to city,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: that on you may come all the righteous blood shed on the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zachariah son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the sanctuary and the altar.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Most certainly I tell you, all these things will come upon this generation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her! How often I would have gathered your children together, even as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, and you would not!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Behold, your house is left to you desolate.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I tell you, you will not see me from now on, until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’
segments.speaker: Jesus
